subheadline: Zergboard gives you a Trello-simple interface with Linear-level structure, multi-tenant workspaces, and an API-first core so your agent flows can create, update, and resolve work automatically.
value_proposition: A lightweight kanban tool built for both human teams and AI agents, with multi-tenant workspaces and an API-first core that lets automations drive ticket workflows.
target_audience: Small-to-mid-size product teams and developer teams who want a simple project board with API access for agent-driven automation, positioned between Trello users who've outgrown it and teams that find Linear too heavy.

metrics: 7 tickets updated from QA failures in the last 15 minutes

pricing.price_anchoring: Free tier lowers barrier to entry; annual billing framed as saving 'roughly 15%'; tiers named to imply growth stages, nudging teams to self-identify their stage and upgrade accordingly

features: Kanban cards and columns with drag-and-drop
Multi-tenant workspaces with organization isolation and membership-based access control
API-first architecture for automations, bots, and agents
Admin superuser panel for platform-level metrics and governance
Webhooks and API tokens (Growth tier and above)
differentiators: Explicitly positioned as 'Trello-simple interface with Linear-level structure'
Built for agent flows — agents can create, update, and resolve work automatically
Multi-tenant by default out of the box
Deploy-ready on Fly.io with portable deployment primitives
trust_signals: SLA-backed support mentioned at Scale tier
Stable, minimal API described as intentionally built for automation

design_patterns.layout: Single-column scrolling landing page with distinct hero, live demo snapshot, features grid, pricing table, and FAQ accordion sections
design_patterns.color_scheme: Appears minimal — likely dark or neutral tones based on technical/developer positioning; no explicit color data in text
design_patterns.typography: Clean, utilitarian — short punchy headlines with longer explanatory subtext
notable_patterns: Live work snapshot section simulating a real board with real-time stats

emotional_tone: Confident and pragmatic — anti-bloat messaging, efficiency-focused, developer-friendly without being cold
key_objections_addressed: Can agents actually use it? (Yes, API is minimal, stable, and built for automation)
Does it support multiple clients/tenants? (Yes, each org is isolated)
Can I self-host if needed? (Yes, Nuxt + Postgres, portable deployment)
Is there a free tier to try before committing? (Yes, up to 3 users free)
missing_or_weak: Zero customer social proof — no logos, testimonials, user counts, or case studies
No credibility signals for the company itself — no founder story, team page, or 'backed by' mentions
No security or compliance signals (SOC2, GDPR, encryption at rest) despite targeting teams with sensitive project data
Live snapshot metrics feel illustrative rather than real, reducing trust impact
No comparison table explicitly showing advantages over Trello or Linear
Scale tier says 'Talk to sales' but no contact method or demo booking is surfaced
No integration ecosystem mentioned (GitHub, Slack, Jira, etc.) which competitors use as a key hook
No screenshot or video of the actual product beyond a text-described snapshot
standout_elements: Sharp positioning wedge — 'Trello-simple, Linear-level structure' immediately orients the buyer without needing a comparison page
Agent-first messaging is genuinely differentiated and timely for the 2024–2025 AI workflow moment
FAQ proactively handles the four most likely objections from a developer/ops buyer
Transparent, graduated pricing with a generous free tier reduces friction for early adoption
Self-hosting option and named deployment stack (Fly.io, Nuxt, Postgres) builds trust with technical buyers
